findingVerification.notes: The benchmark warden.toml explicitly sets defaults.verification.enabled = true.

traceCapture.notes: Run used --traces on all shards. Per-chunk trace metadata is preserved in traceSummaries and the raw JSONL artifacts.

notes: Full benchmark result stitched from one Warden run per corpus SHA using --runtime pi --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-5 --effort high --traces. Each shard analyzes every unique corpus path for that SHA, not the whole Sentry repository. Warden workspace Pi dependencies were updated to @earendil-works/pi-ai/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent 0.80.3 so the local Pi registry exposes Claude Sonnet 5. The benchmark config raises scan limits and the shard validator verifies complete target-file coverage. Raw JSONL artifacts are withheld pending sensitive-data review; committed traceSummaries retain sanitized per-chunk metadata only.

scoring.notes: Agent-verified semantic matches. A finding counts when it identifies the same bug in roughly the same location as an existing corpus finding. Same-file findings about different bugs do not count; one emitted finding may count for multiple corpus entries when it explicitly covers the same shared bug across files.

scores.notes: Same InternalEAFeaturesEndpoint object-permission bypass.
Same inactive-user / disabled OAuth app userinfo bug; does not cover the hashed-token corpus issue.
Secret option logging is not the corpus SYSTEM_OPTIONS_ALLOWLIST tuple-comma bug for this commit.
Timing-unsafe compare finding is not the Statsig timestamp freshness corpus bug.
Bitbucket Cloud IP/signature bypass is not the corpus invalid-signature HMAC logging bug.
Viewer-context SSO/2FA bypass is not the client_secret_jwt exp/jti corpus bug.
Same pull-request size-analysis download project-access bypass.
Same relocation retry status/file-existence disclosure before ownership check.
Same legacy OAuth redirect_uri prefix-match issue.
Same org-level Seer RPC project-access bypass; the separate preprod and anomaly-data corpus entries are not counted unless explicitly found.
Same Seer RPC HMAC URL/path binding issue.
External issue action URL authority injection is not the corpus event-scope bug for this commit.
Same legacy Jira JQL quote/backslash injection.
Same ECharts tooltip HTML injection root cause; the finding anchors mobile builds and carries feature-flag and app-size treemap locations as additional locations.
Covers the Explore saved-query visit permission bypass and explicitly notes the same Discover visit bypass.
Same ExternalTeamDetails org-only lookup IDOR.
Same Slack/Discord identity unlink ownership bug.
Same Jira install JWT issuer/clientKey binding bug.
Same Perforce config-data password exposure.
Same MS Teams action-submitted unchecked groupId bug; the Slack options-load note is not counted as the distinct corpus options disclosure.
Slack Seer Autofix membership/feature check is not a corpus finding for this commit.
Same GCP Pub/Sub data-export notification auth/origin bug.
Same Replay DELETE project:read authorization bug.
Same Autofix run_id-to-group ownership bug.
Workflow attach authorization gap differs from the corpus workflow disconnect permission-check bug.
Sentry App issue-link URL authority injection is not the corpus event JSON access bug.
Same cleartext credential option-value logging bug.
